item.title: Why US shale’s fracking techniques may move offshore to harvest more resources
item.description: Department of Energy’s Kyle Haustveit says hydraulic fracturing could boost recovery rates and extend life of ageing offshore platforms

item.title: Low activity points to US Gulf production decline, executive says
item.description: Beacon Offshore vice president says US Gulf activity at all-time low despite record output

item.title: Kazakh oil company seeks partners as drilling programme proceeds
item.description: Authorities have tasked KazMunayGaz with discovering more oil and gas to underpin future energy security of Kazakhstan

item.title: US contractor reveals slate of new South America deals
item.description: Weatherford wins offshore contracts in Guyana and Brazil

item.title: Talos CEO outlines upcoming exploration plans
item.description: US producer busy with soon-to-start appraisal drilling and progressing new prospects

item.title: Transocean still confident in Valaris merger timeline despite US regulator’s inquiry
item.description: Department of Justice seeks more information on $5.8 billion tie-up

item.title: Transocean chief highlights Africa rig revival
item.description: Keelan Adamson also pinpoints market pickup in Indonesia and India

item.title: Gunvor lands supply deal with US floating LNG project
item.description: Switzerland-headquartered trader continues to expand LNG portfolio

item.title: US not considering further SPR releases, energy official says
item.description: ‘No plan at this time’ to unlock more government reserves, Department of Energy’s Kyle Haustveit tells Upstream

item.title: Go-ahead for new US Gulf oilfield project
item.description: Kosmos also updates on its exploration alliance with Shell

item.title: Kazakhstan readies latest exploration offering
item.description: Four new blocks added to acreage offered during previous rounds

item.title: Oil and gas prices tumble on reports US and Iran nearing peace deal
item.description: Benchmark prices drop by more than 10% in Wednesday trading

item.title: BP to slash stake in flagship UK carbon capture project
item.description: BP holds a 45% share in the Northern Endurance Partnership

item.title: YPF eyes ‘Latin America’s largest project finance’ for Argentina LNG project
item.description: Enterprise will feature newbuild FLNG vessels with gas from Vaca Muerta shale play

item.title: ‘Safer, simpler, faster’: Equinor reveals new operating model
item.description: Norwegian energy giant has started a new operating model

item.title: Consortium finalises $1.1 billion North Africa contract
item.description: Egypt’s Petrojet and Arkad of Italy to build facilities for phase two of Algeria development

item.title: Shell to defer UK partner's development costs again amid takeover talks
item.description: Deltic had deferred payment of its share of development costs to February this year

item.title: EnergyPathways secures UK gas storage licence
item.description: Project would double UK gas storage, according to EnergyPathways

item.title: Adura completes construction of Jackdaw gas field in UK North Sea
item.description: Jackdaw gas field could start supplying UK homes this winter if granted fresh development consent

item.title: Equinor's first-quarter results ahead of expectations
item.description: Results helped by 9% production growth compared to the same quarter last year

item.title: Talos Energy hit by $145 million impairment charge, Q1 losses deepen
item.description: The company produced 88,800 barrels of oil equivalent per day of oil and gas in the first quarter, exceeding its guidance

item.title: Australia gas export tax inquiry set to issue recommendations
item.description: Operators say that a proposed gas export tax would make new projects 'uninvestible'

item.title: Petronas launches drillship re-tender for ultra-deepwater wildcat
item.description: Malaysian operator hopes to spud Redha-1 exploration well offshore Indonesia by October

item.title: Australia and Japan ink energy security cooperation agreement
item.description: The two countries signed a joint statement in “a commitment to mutual energy security and to rules-based and market-oriented trade and investment”

item.title: Australia and Japan sign energy security cooperation agreement
item.description: Countries sign joint statement in “commitment to mutual energy security and to rules-based and market-oriented trade and investment”

item.title: Southeast Asia gas development advances with subsea pipelaying
item.description: First gas from Malampaya phase four project expected in fourth quarter of 2026

item.title: QatarEnergy begins process to repair two LNG mega-trains hit in Iran attack
item.description: QatarEnergy said March attacks damaged two liquefaction trains with combined production capacity of 12.8 million tonnes per annum

item.title: US official defends BOEM-BSEE merger as ‘key component’ of offshore production
item.description: Department of the Interior’s Jacob Tyner says reunification of agencies split after 2010 BP spill will be ‘multi-stage process’

item.title: Senegal starts building pipeline and power plant taking gas from BP asset
item.description: Gas will flow to domestic market from Greater Tortue Ahmeyim (GTA) field, according to partner Kosmos Energy

item.title: Subsea7 awards pre-commissioning job for ultra-deepwater project
item.description: EnerMech secured subsea contract for Trion project offshore Mexico
